# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build:
    name: Build and Cache Dependencies
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: 14
      - name: Cache node_modules
        uses: actions/cache@v3
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
      - name: Install node dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Cache pip packages (for Python)
        uses: actions/cache@v3
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip
      - name: Install pip dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - name: Cache configuration files (if any)
        uses: actions/cache@v3
        with:
          path: ./path-to-configs
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/env/**') }}
      - name: Build the project
        run: npm run build
